task_completion.description: Access and log into the SEB Server
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to http://localhost:8080 showing the SEB Server dashboard or interface, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new exam configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SEB Server interface shows a form or dialog for creating a new Exam Configuration.

task_completion.description: Set the configuration name to 'Literature Analysis Exam'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Literature Analysis Exam' is entered into the configuration name field, or is visible in the list of saved configurations.
A checkbox, toggle, or dropdown for 'Text Search' is visibly checked, toggled on, or set to enabled within the configuration settings.
A checkbox, toggle, or dropdown for 'Right Mouse Button' is visibly checked, toggled on, or set to enabled within the configuration settings.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the SEB Server GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) navigating the UI, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine SEB Server application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface looks like a functional web application with proper styling and layout, not a text editor or a locally created mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no browser Developer Tools panel open showing DOM manipulation to artificially check boxes or change text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected configuration name is exactly 'Literature Analysis Exam'.
